ops.text: CORRECTION + upstream ask on the previous entry (review codex ran with danger-full-access): root cause is codex-ask itself, not the wrapper agent — codex-ask 0.16.0/0.16.1/0.17.0 all HARDCODE `--sandbox danger-full-access` on the `codex exec` invocation with no CLI override (`--help` exposes only -m/-e/-s/--image/--skip-git-repo-check). Hand-rolling `codex exec` is warned against by the skill docs (drops developer_instructions + concurrent-safe temp paths), and patching the plugin cache is silently overwritten on update. Interim mitigation adopted: review lanes verify the target repo's working copy clean (jj st) after every codex exit and report it. Proper fix for the plugin owner: add a `--sandbox` passthrough to codex-ask (or default review-intent invocations to read-only).
